Key Takeaways:
• I wanted to give you a little bonus – skeleton scripts.
• This outlines what to think about as you’re scripting out your own videos.
• You should script them out. Even if you don't deliver them from a script. Even if
you just create the script and write it down and get it all nailed and then go from
an outline or go from memory or don't go from anything, but just script them out
to know what you want to say.
• You’ll find sample language for all the key pieces of your PLC2, PLC2 and PLC3
videos.
• This is just sort of your skeleton script and how to put it together for each one of
the pieces of Prelaunch Content and I definitely would recommend actually
writing out your entire script. Script the entire thing. Whether you deliver it from
a script or not, you want to script the entire thing.

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Okay, so I've already walked you through the three pieces of PLC, but I wanted to
give you a little ... Go a little deeper, a little bonus for you and give you some, what I'm
calling "skeleton scripts."
So this just sort of outlines what to think about as you're scripting out your
videos. I do recommend you script them out. Even if you don't deliver them from a
script. Even if you just create the script and write it down and get it all nailed and then go
from an outline or go from memory or don't go from anything, but just script them out to
know what you want to say.
Don't think you can just turn on the camera and just nail this. Because pretty
much no one can do that. The people that can do that are the people that have been
delivering it for years and years and years and years and years and years. Now they can
just go off of the top of their head.
The way to do this right is to actually script them out. I write out word-for-word -
I write out my entire script these days. Then, how I deliver them, over the years, has been
all over the place. I've sat there and had a script printed out at my feet and I would look
down and I would just memorize two or three sentences and I'd look up and deliver them
into the camera and then look down. That's awkward but I've done it. I've done it on big
whiteboards where I've written out bullet points on whiteboards. I've done it with huge
post-it notes on the wall, behind the camera. Nowadays, for my Prelaunch scripts and
for my sales script, I actually use a teleprompter and I go word-for-word from the
teleprompter.
So there's a lot of different ways you can do this, but you absolutely, whether you
deliver it word-for-word from the script, whether or not ... You still want to script them
out. So you've thought long and hard and again, I know I'm not supposed to be talking
about working hard, but by now you realize that if you're going to completely change
your life with a launch or change your business with a launch, there is some work
involved and so scripting these out, even if you just script them out to bullet point. But I
actually like to go word-for-word.
So PLC1 ... and I'm not going to walk through every ... You know, you don't need
me to read this off for you, right? But I'm just going to walk through it really quickly.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
PLC1, you want to start off with that intro and the positioning. Here's just some
examples. Then you want to show the opportunity. Then you want to share your launch
story. I like to think of this as there's sort of a short version of the story in PLC1 and then
you tend to reflect back on that story or expand on that story as you go through PLC2
and PLC3.
So think about your overall launch story as this one story that arcs through the
three pieces of Prelaunch Content and then you have three individual pieces in each of
the videos that tie in together, but then you also have the first piece, feels like a
standalone piece. Then all of a sudden you have the second video, you have the second
piece, that feels like a standalone piece but builds on that first piece. Then the third piece
is the same. It feels standalone but it builds on those first two pieces and ties this whole
story arc together.
So here we go. Intro and positioning, show the opportunity, share your story or
the launch story, teach, raise objections and answer them right from the beginning, then
you foreshadow PLC2, and then you have your call to action. Now, I go deeper in on this
in the video I shot for you just on PLC1, but I'm just walking through the skeleton scripts
right now.
Okay, walking forward into PLC2. Remember this is the transformation. This is
the "what."
So again, you have introduction and the positioning. The difference here,
between PLC2 and PLC1 is you're recapping the last video. That's the second bullet point,
and tell them what you covered in that first video. You want to do this quickly. You don't
want to be boring. You want it to work for both the people that haven't seen PLC1, but
also the people that watched it. So now you're just sort of reminding them what you
covered. You want to recap the opportunity. Again, you can go look at PLC2. I went into
this deeper, quite a bit deeper on this.
You want to recap your positioning.
You want to present a case study. It could be you as a case study or it could be one
of your case studies and/or you want to do some real teaching in here.
Again, back to the objections, you keep on coming back to that.
A call to action and then foreshadow the third video or the third piece of
Prelaunch Content and then you have a call to action.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Okay, PLC3. This is ownership, the ownership experience. This is the "how."
You have your intro here and then you can do a case study.
There's often a lot of questions here and again, that's a key phrase for objections,
but PLC3, there tends to be more objection crushing, more objection answering in PLC3
than in PLC2 or 1.
You want to give the big view. This is sort of hearkening back to the opportunity
or the journey, but this is the big view of how their life is going to change. What it's going
to be like to not only own your product, but own the transformation that they’re going to
have. The transformation in their life.
Then you want to pivot to the offer and create a soft landing. Again, go watch that
video on ... I walk through exactly how to do this when I walk through PLC3, so definitely
go back and watch that again.
You want to seed the scarcity or the limit on your launch offer and then you want
to have a call to action.
So again, this is just sort of your skeleton script and how to put it together for
each one of the pieces of Prelaunch Content and I definitely would recommend actually
writing out your entire script. Script the entire thing. Whether you deliver it from a script
or not, you want to script the entire thing.
